blocks:
Ayahs

إِذَا زُلْزِلَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ زِلْزَالَهَا (١)
When the earth is rocked with a terrible quake (1)
وَأَخْرَجَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ أَثْقَالَهَا (٢)
and discharges its burdens, (2)
وَقَالَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ مَا لَهَا (٣)
and man says, ‘What is the matter with her?’ (3)
يَوْمَئِذٍ تُحَدِّثُ أَخْبَارَهَا (٤)
On that day she will relate her chronicles (4)
بِأَنَّ رَبَّكَ أَوْحَىٰ لَهَا (٥)
for her Lord will have inspired her. (5)
يَوْمَئِذٍ يَصْدُرُ ٱلنَّاسُ أَشْتَاتًا لِّيُرَوْا۟ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ (٦)
On that day, mankind will issue forth in various groups to be shown their deeds. (6)
فَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْرًا يَرَهُۥ (٧)
So whoever does an atom’s weight of good will see it, (7)
وَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ شَرًّا يَرَهُۥ (٨)
and whoever does an atom’s weight of evil will see it. (8)

(Commentary)

This surah mentions the Resurrection (القيامة) and the departure of people for recompense (الجزاء), alluding to some of its signs (أشراطها), namely the earthquake of the earth and its recounting of its news. The surah could potentially be either Meccan or Medinan.

His saying, the Exalted: إِذا زُلْزِلَتِ الْأَرْضُ زِلْزالَها "When the earth is shaken with its [final] earthquake" (1). The word zilzal (زلزال) is a verbal noun like zilzalah (زلزلة), and its suffixation to the pronoun referring to "the earth" denotes exclusivity (الاختصاص). The meaning is: "When the earth is shaken with its own specific earthquake," which implies magnification and aggrandizement (التعظيم والتفخيم)—that is, an earthquake reaching the ultimate limit of intensity and horror.

His saying, the Exalted: وَأَخْرَجَتِ الْأَرْضُ أَثْقالَها "and the earth discharges its burdens" (2). The word athqal (أثقال) is the plural of thaqal (ثَقَل), meaning belongings or specifically the baggage of a traveler; or it is the plural of thiql (ثِقْل), meaning a load (الحمل). In any case, what is meant by "its burdens" which it discharges are the dead, according to what has been said, or the treasures and minerals within its belly, or all of these. Each view has its proponents, but the first view is the most likely, followed by the third, so that the verse serves as an allusion to their coming forth for the reckoning (الحساب). His saying: يَوْمَئِذٍ يَصْدُرُ النَّاسُ "On that day mankind will issue forth" (6) is an allusion to their departing toward their recompense.

His saying, the Exalted: وَقالَ الْإِنْسانُ ما لَها "and man says, ‘What is the matter with it?’" (3). That is, he says while stunned and amazed by that intense and terrifying earthquake: "What is wrong with the earth that it shakes with such an earthquake?" It is said that "man" here refers to the disbeliever (الكافر) who does not believe in the Resurrection (البعث), and other things have been said as will follow.

His saying, the Exalted: يَوْمَئِذٍ تُحَدِّثُ أَخْبارَها بِأَنَّ رَبَّكَ أَوْحى لَها "On that day it will relate its news [5] because your Lord has inspired it" (4-5). Thus, it shall bear witness to the deeds of the children of Adam, just as their limbs, the record of deeds (كتاب الأعمال) kept by the angels, and the witnesses of deeds (شهداء الأعمال) from among humans and others shall bear witness.

Regarding His saying: بِأَنَّ رَبَّكَ أَوْحى لَها "because your Lord has inspired it," the preposition lam (لـ) carries the meaning of ila (إلى), because inspiration (الإيحاء) is made transitive with ila. The meaning is: "It relates its news because your Lord inspired it to speak." Thus, the earth is sentient (شاعرة) of the deeds—good and evil—that occur upon it, preserving them (متحملة لها), and it is permitted on the Day of Resurrection through revelation (الوحي) to relate its news and testify to what it has carried. It has already been mentioned in the commentary on His saying, the Exalted: وَإِنْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا يُسَبِّحُ بِحَمْدِهِ وَلكِنْ لا تَفْقَهُونَ تَسْبِيحَهُمْ "There is not a thing but celebrates His praise, but you do not understand their glorification" (17:44), and His saying: قالُوا أَنْطَقَنَا اللهُ الَّذِي أَنْطَقَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ "They will say, ‘Allah gave us speech, He who gives speech to all things’" (41:21), that what is understood from the speech of the Glorified is that life (الحياة) and consciousness (الشعور) permeate all things, even if we are heedless of that.

There has been intense disagreement among commentators regarding the meaning of the earth "relating" through revelation: Is it by granting life and consciousness to the dead earth so that it informs of what occurred upon it? Or by creating a sound near it, which is then considered its speech? Or is it its indication through its "state of being" (لسان الحال) regarding the deeds that occurred upon it? There is no place for this disagreement after what you have heard, nor would the proof (الحجة) be established against anyone by such a [metaphorical] type of testimony.

His saying, the Exalted: يَوْمَئِذٍ يَصْدُرُ النَّاسُ أَشْتاتاً لِيُرَوْا أَعْمالَهُمْ "On that day mankind will issue forth in various groups to be shown their deeds" (6). The term sudur (الصدور) refers to the departure of camels from the water after arriving at it. Ashtat (أشتات), like shatta (شتى), is the plural of shatit (شتيت), meaning scattered or diverse. This verse serves as a second consequence to the "when" (إذَا).

What is meant by people issuing forth in various groups on that day is their departure from the station of gathering (الموقف) to their abodes in Paradise or the Fire, with the people of felicity and success (أهل السعادة والفلاح) being distinguished from the people of wretchedness and perdition (أهل الشقاء والهلاك). Their being "shown their deeds" means being shown the recompense of their deeds by entering into it, or witnessing the deeds themselves based on the embodiment of deeds (تجسم الأعمال) — the doctrine that actions take on physical forms in the hereafter.

It is also said: It refers to their coming out of their graves to the station of gathering in diverse groups, distinguished by blackened or whitened faces, and by terror or security, so they may be informed of the recompense of their deeds through the reckoning. In this view, "knowledge" of the recompense is expressed as "seeing," and "informing" is expressed as "showing," similar to His saying, the Exalted: يَوْمَ تَجِدُ كُلَّ نَفْسٍ ما عَمِلَتْ مِنْ خَيْرٍ مُحْضَراً وَما عَمِلَتْ مِنْ سُوءٍ "The day when every soul will find present whatever good it has done; and as for the evil it has done..." (3:30). However, the first view is closer and clearer.

His saying, the Exalted: فَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْراً يَرَهُ وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقالَ ذَرَّةٍ شَرًّا يَرَهُ "So whoever does an atom’s weight of good will see it, [7] and whoever does an atom’s weight of evil will see it" (7-8). The mithqal (المثقال) is that by which weights are measured, and the dharrah (الذرة) refers to the fine dust (الهباء) seen in a sunbeam, and it is also said to refer to small ants.

This is a derivation (تفريع) from what preceded regarding their being shown their deeds. It contains an emphasis of the explanation that no deed—good or evil, large or small—is excluded from being shown, even an atom's weight of good or evil. The state of everyone who does good and evil is explained in two independent sentences for the purpose of providing a criterion (الضابط) and establishing a general rule (القاعدة).

There is no contradiction between the generality (العموم) indicated by these two verses and the verses indicating the nullification of deeds (حبط الأعمال) — the total loss of spiritual reward due to certain sins, or those indicating the transfer of good and evil deeds from one soul to another (such as the good deeds of a murderer going to the victim and the sins of the victim going to the murderer), or those indicating the changing of evil deeds into good deeds for certain repentant ones, and other such matters previously alluded to in the discussion on "Deeds" in the second volume of this book, as well as in the commentary on His saying: لِيَمِيزَ اللهُ الْخَبِيثَ مِنَ الطَّيِّبِ "so that Allah may separate the bad from the good" (8:37).

This is because the aforementioned verses govern (حاكمة) these two verses; for he whose good deed is nullified is judged as not having done good, so he has no good deed to see—and the same logic applies to other cases. So understand.

(Narrative Discussion)

In al-Durr al-Manthur, it is narrated by Ibn Mardawayh and al-Bayhaqi in Shu'ab al-Iman from Anas ibn Malik that the Messenger of Allah صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله said: إن الأرض لتخبر يوم القيامة ـ بكل ما عمل على ظهرها — "Indeed, the earth shall inform on the Day of Resurrection of everything that was done upon its back." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله recited: إِذا زُلْزِلَتِ الْأَرْضُ زِلْزالَها until he reached يَوْمَئِذٍ تُحَدِّثُ أَخْبارَها. He said: "Do you know what its news is? Gabriel came to me and said: 'Its news is that when the Day of Resurrection comes, it shall inform of every deed done upon its back.'"

I say: A similar narration is reported from Abu Hurayrah.

In the same source, it is narrated by al-Husayn ibn Sufyan in his Musnad and Abu Nu'aym in al-Hilyah from Shaddad ibn Aws, who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله saying:

أيها الناس إن الدنيا عرض حاضر ـ يأكل منه البر والفاجر ، وإن الآخرة وعد صادق يحكم فيها ملك قادر ـ يحق فيها الحق ويبطل الباطل. "O people! Indeed, this world is a fleeting commodity (عرض حاضر) from which both the righteous and the profligate eat. And indeed, the Hereafter is a true promise (وعد صادق) in which a Capable King judges; therein He establishes the truth and nullifies falsehood."

أيها الناس كونوا من أبناء الآخرة ـ ولا تكونوا من أبناء الدنيا ـ فإن كل أم يتبعها ولدها ـ اعملوا وأنتم من الله على حذر ، واعلموا أنكم معروضون على أعمالكم ـ وأنكم ملاقوا الله لا بد منه ـ فمن يعمل مثقال ذرة خيرا يره ـ ومن يعمل مثقال ذرة شرا يره. "O people! Be children of the Hereafter and do not be children of this world, for every mother is followed by her child. Work while you are in a state of godwariness (حذر) of Allah, and know that you shall be presented before your deeds and that you shall inevitably meet Allah: 'So whoever does an atom’s weight of good will see it, and whoever does an atom’s weight of evil will see it.'"

In Tafsir al-Qummi, regarding His saying, the Exalted: وَأَخْرَجَتِ الْأَرْضُ أَثْقالَها, he said: "From among the people." Regarding وَقالَ الْإِنْسانُ ما لَها, he said: "That is the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) عليه‌السلام." Regarding يَوْمَئِذٍ تُحَدِّثُ أَخْبارَها until His saying أَشْتاتاً, he said: "They come in various groups: believers, disbelievers, and hypocrites." Regarding لِيُرَوْا أَعْمالَهُمْ, he said: "They shall stand over what they have done."

In the same source, in a narration by Abu al-Jarud from Abu Ja'far عليه‌السلام regarding His saying: فَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْراً يَرَهُ, he said: "If he is from the people of the Fire and had done an atom's weight of good in the world, it shall be [a source of] regret (حسرة) for him on the Day of Resurrection if his deed was for other than Allah." Regarding وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقالَ ذَرَّةٍ شَرًّا يَرَهُ, he said: "If he is from the people of Paradise, he shall see that evil on the Day of Resurrection, and then he will be forgiven."
